etymology.description: semantic 水(水) (shuǐ, "water") + phonetic 睪(睪) (yì) -> marsh; favor; grace
etymology.interpretation: The character 澤 (zé, "marsh; favor; grace") combines the semantic element 水(水) (shuǐ, "water"), connecting to the wet and marshy nature of a swamp, with the phonetic element 睪(睪) (yì) providing the sound. By extension, just as water spreads favorably over the land in a marsh, the character also conveys the ideas of favor and grace.
etymology.simplification: Simplified as 泽 by replacing 睪(睪) (yì) with the simpler 𠬤.

examples.chinese: 澤(澤)是一片充满生机的湿地(是一片充滿生機的濕地)。
examples.pinyin: Zé shì yī piàn chōngmǎn shēngjī de shīdì.
examples.english: A marsh is a vibrant wetland.

examples.chinese: 他在公司中享有很大的恩澤(他在公司中享有很大的恩澤)。
examples.pinyin: Tā zài gōngsī zhōng xiǎngyǒu hěn dà de ēnzé.
examples.english: He enjoys great favor in the company.

examples.chinese: 古老的城市依偎在河流的澤邊(古老的城市依偎在河流的澤邊)，仿佛在聆听大自然的呢喃(彷彿在聆聽大自然的呢喃)。
examples.pinyin: Gǔlǎo de chéngshì yīwēi zài héliú de zé biān, fǎngfú zài língtīng dàzìrán de nénán.
examples.english: The ancient city snuggled by the river's marsh, as if listening to the whispers of nature.
